data.title: Traws stock rallies 24% on avian flu drug update
data.description:
Shares of Traws Pharma (NASDAQ:TRAW) shot up 24% in post-market trading Friday on encouraging results from a Phase 1 study of the company's drug Tivoxavir Marboxil in the treatment of H5N1 avian flu.

The study showed a single dose of the drug maintained plasma drug levels above the effective concentration, EC90, for over 23 days, indicating the potential for sustained efficacy with a one-time administration. No treatment-related adverse events were reported, according to a statement.

The company said plans were underway to initiate a Phase 2 study of the product in the first half of 2025.

data.title: Calumet closes $1.44B DoE loan for Montana renewable fuels, biomass energy plant
data.description:
Calumet (NASDAQ:CLMT) +8.1% post-market Friday after saying it closed a $1.44B guaranteed loan with the U.S. Department of Energy's Loan Programs Office to build and expand the renewable fuels facility owned by its Montana Renewables subsidiary.

Calumet (CLMT) said the expansion will position Montana Renewables as one of the world's largest sustainable aviation fuel producers, enabling an increase in production capacity to 300M gal/year of SAF and 330M gal/year of combined SAF and renewable diesel.

The initial loan proceeds of $782M are expected to be funded this month, with the balance of proceeds funded through the planned construction period, the company said.

"Following our conversion to a C-corporation, we emphasized the importance of the DoE loan as the next major step in Calumet's vision, and achieving that milestone is a tremendous accomplishment," CEO Todd Borgmann said.

data.title: Apple CEO compensation increases $10M in 2024
data.description:
Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) CEO Tim Cook's year-over-year total compensation will increase by $10M for 2024, contingent on how shareholders vote at next month's annual meeting, according to an SEC filing on Friday.

Cook's total target CEO compensation was $59M. This includes a base pay of $3M, a cash incentive plan of $6M and a target equity award of $50M.

"Upon review of the relevant primary peer CEO benchmark data for 2024, Mr. Cook's 2023 pay would have been positioned near the low end of the desired range," the filing reads. "The People and Compensation Committee recognized the growth and success Mr. Cook delivered to shareholders for more than a decade as CEO and the impact his leadership has on the company's short-term and long-term success. As a result, the People and Compensation Committee determined to continue to emphasize long-term shareholder alignment and increase the target value of his equity award for 2024 from $40 million to $50 million."

Cook's base salary of $3M has remained the same since 2016.

Investors will vote on the proposed compensation of Cook and other executives, along with several outside proposals, at the 2025 annual shareholders meeting at 11 a.m. ET on February 25.

The outside proposals include a report on ethical AI data acquisition and usage, a report on costs and benefits of child sex abuse material-identifying software and user privacy, a request to cease DEI efforts and a report on charitable giving. The board voted against all of these measures.

Regarding AI data acquisition, Apple said the "requested report is unnecessary given Apple already provides all the information requested regarding Apple’s AI data privacy practices."

As for the child sex abuse issue, Apple said it believes "that our current approach to child safety, which is informed by stakeholder engagement, is more appropriate than the universal surveillance suggested in the proposal, which could have serious implications for our users’ human and civil rights globally."

In the case of the proposal wanting the company to cease DEI efforts, the board stated, "Apple has a well-established compliance program and the proposal inappropriately attempts to restrict Apple's ability to manage its own ordinary business operations, people and teams, and business strategies."

And for the charitable giving report, Apple responded it "has a well-established corporate donations program that follows a strict internal governance and approval process, and the proposal attempts to inappropriately restrict Apple’s ability to manage its own ordinary business operations and business strategies."

data.title: Gold gains despite strong U.S. jobs data as traders feast on Trump uncertainty
data.description:
Gold further gained Friday, capping a strong week as uncertainty around future U.S. policy under President-elect Trump sparked safe-haven demand, even as stronger than forecast U.S. jobs data reinforced expectations the Federal Reserve might not cut interest rates as aggressively this year.

Gold jumped after the release of the jobs report, which showed 256K jobs being added in the U.S. during December, much more than expected by the market, while the unemployment rate unexpectedly ticked lower to 4.1%; the U.S. dollar index jumped following the report, and the 30-year Treasury yield rose above 5% for the first time in more than a year.

“Gold’s strength following a robust jobs report might seem counterintuitive, as it typically suggests a stronger dollar and higher yields, which are headwinds for gold,” Alex Ebkarian of Allegiance Gold wrote. “But this reaction highlights a broader unease among investors - it reflects deeper fears about structural issues like unsustainable debt levels, overvalued markets, and geopolitical uncertainty.”

Gold's resilience is “impressive considering the stronger dollar and rising bond yields,” according to Saxo Bank head of commodities strategy Ole Hansen, indicating the metal is “driven by other factors including trade wars, inflation worries, and fiscal debt worries.”

Financial market expectations have moved to price in a single rate cut no sooner than June that would mark the end of the Fed's rate-cutting cycle.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for January delivery ended +0.9% Friday and up 2.4% for the week at $2,708.50/oz, and front-month January silver (XAGUSD:CUR) settled +1% on Friday and up 4.3% for the week to $31.091/oz.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

"Dollar strength, rising Treasury yields and a rising gold price are all evidence of global concerns with the U.S. fiscal situation," Gold Newsletter editor Brien Lundin said this week.

Gold is "the ultimate safe haven, and is therefore being bought by a growing cohort of investors from central banks down to individual investors... "Gold has been performing impressively against the supposed headwinds of rising yields and dollar strength," and that performance is likely to continue, Lundin said.

data.title: Rumble announces cloud agreement with government of El Salvador
data.description:
• Rumble (RUM) said on Friday that it has entered a cloud services agreement with the Government of El Salvador. 
  • Rumble’s services to El Salvador will include cloud storage, cloud computing, databases, load balancers, and Kubernetes integration.
  • Rumble (RUM) shares were up more than 11% in postmarket trading on Friday.

data.title: Aspargo Labs files to go public via direct offering
data.description:
Aspargo Labs (AAGO), which develops liquid formulations of solid dose drugs and drug delivery devices, has filed to go public via a direct offering.

Shareholders plan to offer up to 177M shares for the offering. The company will not receive any proceeds from the deal. In a private transaction in Q3 2024, 21M common shares were sold for $0.87 per share.

Aspargo (AAGO) hopes to list its shares on NYSE under the symbol AAGO.

Based in New York, Aspargo's (AAGO) lead product is a liquid formulation of sildenafil citrate, the active ingredient in Viagra, that's administered via a device that allows for customized dosing. The product is approved in Spain, the United Kingdom, Germany, the Netherlands and Ireland.

data.title: Brewers turn defensive as Constellation sounds sobering alarm on demand
data.description:
Brewer and spirit stocks suffered steep losses on Friday after Constellation Brands (STZ) warned of “subdued” spending and value seeking behavior among legal-age consumers, sending shares of Modelo’s parent company down by double-digits, and dragging down the sector in sympathy.

Although Constellation (STZ) said its beer business continued to outperform all categories, CEO Bill Newlands reiterated on the company’s Q3 earnings call that “subdued overall spending behaviors from consumers have been key near-term limiting factors on demand growth along with value seeking behavior,” a sentiment that spooked investors and weighed on shares of The Boston Beer Company (SAM), Anheuser-Busch (BUD), Molson Coors Beverage (TAP), Ambev (ABEV), Brown-Forman (BF-A, BF-B), Diageo (DEO), and MGP Ingredients (MGPI), all of which closed on Friday with losses ranging from 2% to 7%.

The loss was particularly pronounced for shares of The Boston Beer Company (SAM) which shed 7% on Friday, making it the fifth day in a row the stock has closed in the red and its fourth consecutive week lower. The company has struggled with lower shipping volumes and sluggish demand for its Truly Hard Seltzer, and is expected to post a loss of $1.27 per share when it reports Q4 results on February 11. Shares for the parent company of Sam Adams are down 22% year-over-year.

And it's not just Boston Beer (SAM) facing challenges. According to the Brewers’ Association, beer consumption per capita was down in 2024, especially for craft brewers as customers shifted to less expensive and more consistent brands, making last year the eighth consecutive year of declining consumption. Demand for non-alcoholic brands is also stealing market share as its popularity gains traction among younger, health-conscious consumers. Industry data estimates demand for non-alcoholic, or reduced alcohol beer to increase at a CAGR of 7% over the next 10 years and likely grow further on the heels of a Surgeon General advisory on the link between alcohol and cancer.

The warning spooked investors and sent beer, wine, and spirit shares lower in its wake.

In the aftermath of Q3 results, shares of Constellation Brands (STZ) fell 17%, making Friday its worst day in a decade.

data.title: NioCorp Developments reports preliminary net loss of $0.5 million
data.description:
• NioCorp Developments (NASDAQ:NB) on Friday reported a preliminary loss of $0.5 million, or $0.01 per share, for the three-month period ended December 31, 2024, compared to a loss of $3.3 million, or $0.09 per share, in the same period last year.
  • Preliminary net loss for the six-month period ended December 31, 2024, of $2.6 million, or $0.06 per share, said the company.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Matinas BioPharma receives NYSE noncompliance notice
data.description:
• Matinas BioPharma (NYSE:MTNB) said on Friday that it has received an NYSE American notice indicating that the company has fallen below certain NYSE American continued listing standards because it failed to hold its annual meeting of stockholders for the fiscal year ending on December 31, 2024.
  • The company intends to hold its annual meeting no later than December 31, 2025 to regain compliance under the NYSE American Company Guide.
  • Matinas BioPharma (NYSE:MTNB) shares were down 3.21% in post market trading on Friday.

data.title: General European Strategic Investments announces passing of CEO Wolfgang Rauball
data.description:
• General European Strategic Investments (OTCPK:GESI) on Friday announced the passing of CEO Wolfgang Rauball.
  • The company said its board of directors has begun the process of identifying a suitable successor to lead General European Strategic Investments.
  • Source: Press Release
